figure.title: PCS Processing Pipeline and FEC Details
figure.purpose: Provides a detailed view of the data transformations, sizes, and FEC encoding applied in the PCS transmit path.

technical_summary: Four 66b words (264 bits total) are transcoded via a 64B/66B to 256B/257B transcoder, resulting in a 257-bit 'tx_coded' block.
The 257-bit block is scrambled using an X^58 scrambler to produce 'tx_scrambled'.
Alignment Markers (AM) are overwritten to produce 'tx_scrambled_am'.
These form a message (m543 to m0) which is fed into an RS-Encode (Reed-Solomon) block.
The output codeword includes parity (c543 to c0) and is then distributed into 10-bit symbols across 8 lanes (Lane 0 to 7).

components.name: X^58 scrambler
components.description: Scrambles data for DC balance and transition density.

critical_facts: The PCS utilizes standard high-speed Ethernet-like techniques: 256B/257B transcoding for efficiency, X^58 scrambling, and Reed-Solomon FEC distributed over 8 lanes.
